summary: Systems review cannot accept SIM-003 yet. GameCommand validation, shared Node/Worker canary path, structured rejection, and deterministic hash parity are broadly aligned with ADR-004, but Query v1 currently leaks a mutable WorldState child object through the district summary read model. In packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts, sim.list-district-summaries returns state.control by reference for existing district state; mutating the query result can mutate runtime.world.state outside submitCommandV1, violating the single-writer authority boundary and the read-model-safe Query v1 acceptance criterion.

tests_run.result: Vitest passed: Test Files 5 passed (5), Tests 26 passed (26). Existing tests do not catch the Query read-model aliasing issue.
Vitest passed: Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 3 passed (3).
Node hash 61e0ba1c matched Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; Node command/query hash d275faa4 matched Worker-compatible command/query hash d275faa4.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.

After booting minimal-m1, applying debug.set-district-control, querying sim.list-district-summaries, and mutating q.result.districts[0].control.controllerPolityId to 999, runtime.world.state.districts[0].control.controllerPolityId also became 999; validateWorldStateV0 then reported bad-reference and hash-mismatch.

decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES rather than ACCEPT because the read-model query boundary must not expose mutable WorldState internals.
No new ADR is required for the current fix if it remains a DTO-copy/test correction within ADR-004 and SIM-003 scope.
risks: The current test named read-model-safe only checks that definitions/state are absent as top-level properties; it does not prove object-reference isolation.
Duplicate command detection remains runtime-scoped and not persisted in WorldState or save/replay tail; this was acknowledged by the writer and can remain deferred to snapshot/save v1 if documented.
blockers: Fix packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts so sim.list-district-summaries returns fresh serializable DTO objects for nested control state instead of state.control references.
Add a regression test that mutating a query result or delta/read-model object cannot mutate runtime.world and cannot invalidate validateWorldStateV0(runtime.world).
requested_action: Clone/copy nested Query v1 read-model DTO state at the boundary, add regression coverage for read-model alias isolation, rerun the affected SIM-003 gates, and return REVIEW handoff to systems_architect.
